title: Provider Runtime / Adapter SSOT: Public Migration Status

loop.observation: Provider public migration status already generated reader docs and package evidence, but the manifest was not counted as a direct evidence loop.
loop.hypothesis: Declaring the migration-status loop makes provider package migration drift visible without folding this special evidence into the provider-runtime wrapper.
loop.execute: Generate public migration docs, verify provider pages, implementation package paths, workflow evidence, and migration boundary assertions.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when generated docs, provider ids, implementation artifacts, source files, or workflow wiring drift.
loop.retrospective: Migration status stays a focused direct loop because skipped provider gates and package moves need their own evidence semantics.

summary: This manifest tracks which Provider Runtime / Adapter surfaces have moved into public riido-daemon.
The reader docs are generated so migration status cannot drift from package, gate, and boundary evidence.

pages.title: Scope Boundaries
summary: RIID-4651 moved internal/agentbridge into public riido-daemon.
The root is stdlib-only and provider-neutral: Adapter, RawEvent / Parser, RunState, reducer, telemetry parser, and tool start gate.
facts: Task DB, project, MWSD, local API, server, control-plane, infra, secrets, and state files were not included in this slice.
Figma onboarding planning screens remain outside C4 ownership.
C4 may report runtime detection and liveness, then execute SaaS-authorized assignments, but it does not own UI labels, fixture catalog, direct-setting forms, workspace selectors, or preview popovers.
Node 432:46849 may collect agent draft/configuration before runtime and workspace selection; C4 consumes only final authorized runtime/model/instruction snapshots.
Web onboarding node 236:29749 is outside C4; download CTA may lead to daemon artifact execution, but sign-up, terms, invite, waitlist, marketing consent, and progress references remain upstream.

pages.title: Core Packages
summary: Core provider-neutral packages moved before concrete provider adapters.
These rows are migration evidence, not permission to move adjacent SaaS, task DB, project, or infra responsibilities into C4.
table_rows: bounded/redacted ToolRef.Args, C7 risk surface, AutoApprover, ToolStartGate
one-run session actor, parser/driver/reducer stream, timeout, cancellation, telemetry, fail-closed gate
provider registry, detect/run entrypoint, PATH lookup, env override pin, version probe
mailbox actor, detect, capability reconciliation, bounded slots, submit/cancel/status/heartbeat
provider-neutral source/reporter port for registration, heartbeat, claim, cancel-watch, events
facts: Provider-native hook/RPC pre-start interrupt and SaaS/web approval handoff are executed by later session/runtimeactor/provider adapter slices, not by the early tool policy slice alone.
Still outside these moves: supervisor polling loop, controlplane/saasplane, controlplane/taskdbplane, concrete provider adapters, server HTTP/SSE transport, task DB, project, and MWSD adapters.

pages.title: Claude Adapter
summary: RIID-4658 moved internal/provider/claude into public riido-daemon.
The package does not bundle Claude Code CLI.
facts: It owns external executable detection, command construction, stream-json parser, raw event translator, stdin protocol driver, and provider input approval frame builder.
Real Claude CLI execution is gated by AGENTBRIDGE_INTEGRATION=1.
A-51 added an integration gate that expects ResultCompleted and an expected file artifact inside daemon-selected workdir.
The gate requires local Claude Code auth/runtime and explicitly uses PermissionModeAcceptEdits to allow edit/write tools.
A skipped gate does not prove filesystem side effects.

pages.title: Codex Adapter
summary: RIID-4659 moved internal/provider/codex into public riido-daemon.
The package does not bundle Codex CLI.
facts: It owns codex --sandbox danger-full-access app-server --listen stdio:// construction, daemon-owned full-access runtime selection, JSONL parser, raw event translator, JSON-RPC protocol driver, pending request actor, and approval response path.
Codex app-server can use the user's existing Codex auth store.
Workdir is a daemon-selected task/evidence root, not a filesystem sandbox boundary.
The full-access launch shape is C4 adapter-owned harness policy, not provider default or caller input.
A-57 added a real CLI gate that expects ResultCompleted and an expected file artifact inside daemon-selected workdir.
Codex runtime model catalog may report host Codex config model as runtime-scoped opaque model_id.
Daemon never infers model catalog from OpenAI/ChatGPT tokens, account identity, API key, team id, or Open API key.
Control-plane fallback catalog ids are preserved in assignment metadata but normalized to StartRequest.Model=""; provider native flags need real provider model ids.

pages.title: OpenClaw Adapter
summary: RIID-4660 moved internal/provider/openclaw into public riido-daemon.
The package does not bundle OpenClaw CLI.
facts: It owns external executable detection, calendar-version gate, openclaw agent --local --json command construction, mandatory session id resolution, JSON/NDJSON parser, and raw event translator.
Real OpenClaw execution is gated by AGENTBRIDGE_INTEGRATION=1.
OpenClaw --session-id is provider-native.
Existing ResumeSessionID is preserved, but first-run TaskID fallback is normalized to riido-<sanitized-task-slug>-<short-hash>.
A-42 showed SaaS thread completion can happen without expected workdir artifact, so text completion is not filesystem side-effect evidence.
A-48 added an integration gate that expects ResultCompleted and an expected file artifact in daemon-selected workdir.
If the gate skips, filesystem side effect is not proven.
OpenClaw agent currently lacks per-run --workspace / --cwd.
Tasks requiring daemon-selected workdir are pre-submit blocked with required_surfaces=["worktree"].
Capability reconciliation exposes OpenClaw SupportsWorktree=false and Claude/Codex/Cursor SupportsWorktree=true.

pages.title: Cursor Adapter
summary: RIID-4661 moved internal/provider/cursor into public riido-daemon.
The package does not bundle Cursor Agent CLI.
facts: It owns root-print, agent-subcommand, legacy-chat launch profile selection, --yolo unsafe bypass policy gate, daemon task workdir headless workspace trust acknowledgement --trust, external executable detection, stream-json parser, and raw event translator.
Cursor --trust prevents an interactive trust prompt for daemon-selected workdir.
--trust is not tool auto-approval.
--yolo remains an unsafe bypass surface and can be used only after C7 policy gate approval.
Real Cursor Agent execution is gated by AGENTBRIDGE_INTEGRATION=1.

assertions: public migration status reader docs are generated from this manifest
each concrete provider row has a unique provider_id and existing implementation package
integration gates are evidence surfaces and skipped gates do not prove filesystem side effects
migration status does not transfer UI, SaaS, task DB, project, infra, secret, or state ownership into C4
